title: CASE WHEN Expression
content:
### CASE WHEN Expression

CASE WHEN is SQL's way of implementing conditional logic (if-then-else) within queries. It returns a value based on specified conditions.

---

### Simple CASE Syntax

```sql
SELECT name,
       CASE department
           WHEN 'Engineering' THEN 'Tech'
           WHEN 'Sales' THEN 'Revenue'
           ELSE 'Other'
       END AS category
FROM employees;
```

### Searched CASE Syntax

```sql
SELECT name, salary,
       CASE
           WHEN salary >= 100000 THEN 'High'
           WHEN salary >= 50000 THEN 'Medium'
           WHEN salary IS NULL THEN 'Unknown'
           ELSE 'Low'
       END AS salary_level
FROM employees;
```

### CASE in WHERE Clause

```sql
SELECT name, salary
FROM employees
WHERE
    CASE WHEN department = 'Engineering' THEN 1
         WHEN department = 'Sales' THEN 1
         ELSE 0
    END = 1;
```

### CASE in ORDER BY

```sql
SELECT name, department, salary
FROM employees
ORDER BY
    CASE
        WHEN department = 'Engineering' THEN 1
        WHEN department = 'Sales' THEN 2
        ELSE 3
    END;
```

### CASE with Aggregation

```sql
SELECT department,
       COUNT(*) AS total,
       SUM(CASE WHEN salary > 75000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_earners,
       AVG(CASE WHEN active = 1 THEN salary END) AS avg_active_salary
FROM employees
GROUP BY department;
```

> **Key Point:** CASE evaluates conditions in order and returns the first match. If no condition is true and there is no ELSE, it returns NULL.
codeExample:
SELECT name,
       salary,
       CASE
           WHEN salary < 50000 THEN 'Junior'
           WHEN salary < 80000 THEN 'Mid'
           ELSE 'Senior'
       END AS level
FROM employees
ORDER BY salary;

quiz.question: What does CASE return if no condition matches and there is no ELSE?
Can CASE be used in ORDER BY?
